vulnerability.reference: Clinicians use coherent-sounding concept maps as cognitive crutch, ignoring potential visual-textual misalignment.
vulnerability.generated: Clinicians' inability to detect inconsistencies between visual explanations and underlying images due to time pressure and automation bias.

impact.reference: Erroneous differential diagnosis from falsely attributed clinical concepts.
impact.generated: Diagnostic errors and inappropriate treatments from plausible but incorrect explanations, harming patients and eroding trust in AI.

dose_response.reference: Joint segmentation and rationale generation models producing inconsistent textual justifications enable false concept-attribution maps, which in clinical concept grounding expose clinicians to erroneous differential diagnoses, leading to unnecessary follow-up imaging or invasive tests.
dose_response.generated: Segmentation-rationale models producing visually grounded but possibly inconsistent justifications, through clinician review of concept-attribution maps, trigger inability to detect mismatches under time pressure and automation bias, leading to diagnostic errors from misleading explanations, harming patients and eroding trust.
